steps.title: Construct the confidence interval
steps.content: Subtract and add the margin of error: $0.49-0.04=0.45$ and $0.49+0.04=0.53$. So the plausible range is 0.45 to 0.53.

steps.title: Interpret what the interval means
steps.content: Any proportion from 0.45 to 0.53 is plausible. Proportions below 0.45 or above 0.53 are not supported by the sample data.

steps.title: Evaluate all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It is plausible that the proportion is between $0.45$ and $0.53$.: This exactly describes the interval. ✓
  • Choice B — It is plausible that the proportion is less than $0.45$.: Values below 0.45 are outside the interval. ✗
  • Choice C — The proportion is exactly $0.49$.: 0.49 is the point estimate, not guaranteed to be the true population proportion. ✗
  • Choice D — It is plausible that the proportion is greater than $0.53$.: Values above 0.53 are outside the interval. ✗
That matches Choice A — It is plausible that the proportion is between $0.45$ and $0.53$..
